channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct
item.description: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and his "overbearing" behaviour.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Albert Manifold succeed Helge Lund at BP last year.

item.title: 'Kidding ourselves': Daley whacks talk of Cleary's losing Origin record
item.description: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

media:group.media:description: The pressure is on Nathan Cleary to reverse a losing Origin career record as this year's campaign gets underway.

item.title: Live: ASX to fall ahead of inflation data, Nasdaq hits record high
item.description: The Australian share market is likely to start its day relatively flat after Iran accused the United States of violating their fragile ceasefire and warned of retaliation. The key focus will be today's inflation data from the ABS, which economists expect to show inflation easing slightly to around 4.4 per cent due to cheaper petrol prices. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: David Chau

media:group.media:description: The ASX 200 is still near record highs despite its recent falls.

item.title: Student says WA school closed by toxic baits had been overrun by mice
item.description: A year 12 student at the WA College of Agriculture Morawa says there were fears of "cooking" mice in the heating system before the mistaken deployment of toxic baits shut the school down.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Henville

media:group.media:description: The deployment of toxic mouse baits has closed the WA College of Agriculture in Morawa.

item.title: Rain brings potential 'opportunities' in renewed search for Gus Lamont, police say
item.description: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Police have launched a renewed search at Oak Park Station for missing four-year-old Gus Lamont.

item.title: Brad Scott reveals he wanted Essendon to trade Zach Merrett, confirms bumper Harley Reid offer
item.description: The ousted Essendon coach reveals he was against the decision to keep Zach Merrett at the club despite a late trade offer from Hawthorn.

media:group.media:description: Brad Scott (left) revealed he wanted Essendon to trade Zach Merrett (right) to Hawthorn last summer.

item.title: Australian underdog stuns former world number one at French Open
item.description: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

media:group.media:description: Adam Walton celebrates with the crowd after downing Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

item.title: After two decades of addiction, drug court offered Brandie a way out
item.description: The peer support worker at Dubbo's Bunmabunmarra service knows firsthand how the drug court can offer a second chance.
item.dc:creator: Lily Plass

media:group.media:description: Brandie McGrady says she has turned her life around in the past two years.

item.title: Live: Jobseeker services to get first overhaul in 30 years
item.description: Employment Minister Amanda Rishworth will today announce major changes to Australia's unemployment system, which currently supports more than 1 million jobseekers. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Social Services Minister Amanda Rishworth speaks at a press conference in Sydney.

item.title: Two prospective Whyalla steelworks buyers remain, as sale reaches 'final stages'
item.description: The federal and state governments have reduced the number of bidders for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks from five to two.

media:group.media:description: Only two bidders remain in the race to buy the Whyalla steelworks.

item.title: 'Can't put it into words': Origin star's mum reveals heartbreaking call
item.description: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Kelly

media:group.media:description: A tearful Queensland coach Billy Slater hugs skipper Cameron Munster after last year's Origin decider.

item.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet slowly restored
item.description: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

media:group.media:description: Israel has increased strikes and ground operations in Lebanon.

item.title: ACT public school system to be unified in 'generational shift'
item.description: The ACT's public education system will be significantly reformed after a review of resourcing found schools operated much like "independent small businesses".
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: The review of public school system resourcing recommended implementing a unified approach to the system.

item.title: Rare hybrid turtle hatchlings discovered on Queensland beach
item.description: Rare baby turtles that are a cross between the loggerhead and green species have been found on the beach at Mon Repos.
item.dc:creator: Nikki Sorbello

media:group.media:description: Two of the 15 hybrids found have been sent to Sea World for further research.

item.title: 'No excuse' for renewable company destroying Wiradjuri rock shelter
item.description: Members of a First Nations community in Central West NSW say they were devastated to learn a culturally significant rock shelter had been destroyed to make way for a renewable energy project.
item.dc:creator: Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: The rock shelter near the Cope State Forest in the Mudgee district that was destroyed by ACEREZ workers.

item.title: Murderer convicted of 1999 crime back in custody for breaching parole
item.description: Murderer Garry Thompson has been returned to custody for 12 months for breaching his parole conditions multiple times.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Garry Thompson was jailed for life by South Australia's Supreme Court for the 1999 murder of Jodi-Ann Trueman.

item.title: Fidel Castro's daughter hopes US can bring down father's regime
item.description: Fidel Castro's daughter says the US has failed to find a way to deal with Cuba for decades. But she tells the ABC that the Trump administration has a difference that could spell the end of the regime she fled.
item.dc:creator: Kamin Gock and Phoebe Hosier

media:group.media:description: Alina Fernández with her father Fidel Castro on her wedding day.

item.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops
item.description: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.
item.dc:creator: Laurissa Smith and Bec Pridham

media:group.media:description: It is hoped that an industry can be developed to make use of the eleven-armed seastar.

item.title: SAPOL commissioner rejects outback police shortage plan
item.description: Commissioner Grant Stevens rejects recommendations to attract more officers to the understaffed Coober Pedy station over funding concerns.
item.dc:creator: Declan Durrant and George Yankovich

media:group.media:description: Coober Pedy, about 645km south of Alice Springs, and about 850km north of Adelaide, is experiencing police staffing shortages and retention issues.

item.title: Tasmania's Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary to star in Paramount+ series
item.description: It's not all cute and fluffy animal stories at Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary. Its director Greg Irons says a new docuseries will show the real face of animal conservation, and it's not all "happy Larry".
item.dc:creator: Ebony ten Broeke

media:group.media:description: Fraser Johnston filming Greg Irons during the production of docuseries, 'The Animal Sanctuary'.

item.title: Why drones fell from the sky during Sydney's Vivid light show
item.description: The company behind the drone shows at Sydney's Vivid festival said spectators were not at risk when almost 90 unmanned aircraft fell from the sky during a display on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: Almost 90 drones crashed into Sydney Harbour and nearby during a Vivid light show.

item.title: Department accused of 'poor decision-making' over $6.2m helmet rollout
item.description: Queensland's fire minister has intervened following tensions between the firefighter union and department.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: The then-Palaszczuk government announced the rollout of the jet-style helmets in 2023 to "improve frontline communication".

item.title: Rental crisis puts domestic violence victim-survivors in 'diabolical' situation
item.description: As Brisbane's rental market hits record high prices and historic low vacancy rates, advocates are warning some domestic violence victims are having to choose between staying in an unsafe home and facing homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bahr

media:group.media:description: Many women struggle to find safe, affordable housing after leaving domestic violence.

item.title: Reception hours slashed at police stations in Melbourne's outer-north
item.description: Victoria Police says its decision to cut the hours police stations are open to the public in Melbourne's outer north will result in more police on the frontline.
item.dc:creator: Margaret Paul

media:group.media:description: Victoria Police says the reduction in reception staffing will allow for more frontline policing.

item.title: Grants allocated for soybean and rice trials in Mossman
item.description: The Queensland government will allocate $300,000 for new soybean and rice projects in Mossman as the region continues to search for a future beyond sugar cane.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson and Brendan Mounter

media:group.media:description: Drew Watson has seen good results from his first commercial soybean crop, despite the prolonged wet season.
